title: Exercise versioning: ship a v2 of one protocol with a v1-to-v2 diff/changelog
context: Versioning discipline — semantic versioning with a human-readable changelog and a reviewable diff between versions — is a core project quality bar and M2 exit criterion. This task exercises that discipline by bumping one already-shipped seed protocol to v2 with a substantive, reviewer-approved change, producing the first v1-to-v2 diff in the corpus. Since the protocol has safety content, the revision requires a safety re-review and adopter notification per the contribution workflow. The riskTier is high because any change to safety content triggers full safety re-review.
objective: Select one shipped seed protocol, make a substantive, documented revision to produce v2.0.0, generate a reviewer-approved v1-to-v2 changelog and diff, perform a safety re-review of the changes, and notify known adopters per the Sev-1 errata / revision workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: A shipped seed protocol is bumped to a new semver (e.g., v2.0.0) with a clear, substantive change documented in CHANGELOG.md.
A v1-to-v2 diff (git diff or equivalent) is produced and committed alongside the new version.
Safety re-review performed on the revision; any changed safety content signed off by the credentialed safety reviewer.
Known adopters notified of the revision per the contribution workflow (workflow-011); notification record committed.
The updated protocol passes validate and lint; rendered Markdown/HTML updated; CI green; commit DCO signed-off.
resources: lab-protocols-open workflow-011 (contribution workflow and errata/revision process)
output: A committed v2 protocol record with CHANGELOG.md, v1-to-v2 diff, safety re-review sign-off, adopter notification record, updated rendered outputs; CI green; commit DCO signed-off.
